spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates font-variant-emoji: text protecting symbol-font codepoints from auto-flipping to colour emoji. Concrete motivating use case from the spec discussion.

interactive.rationale: Live-edit input feeds four variant columns; real interactive surface beyond static columns.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises live-edit + four values + icon-font strip — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show what happens when a glyph is supported by NEITHER an emoji font nor a text font (fallback box). Doesn't include actual icon font (e.g. Material Symbols woff2) so the visitor only sees system fallback.

references.rationale: See also has no chromestatus.com link, no spec link — sibling concept only. CLAUDE.md rule #3 regression.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link
openQuestions.detail: Rule #3 self-heal requires https://chromestatus.com/feature/6566092561973248 in see-also. Currently missing.

openQuestions.title: Embed a real icon font
openQuestions.detail: Symbol-font use case lands much harder with an actual icon font loaded (Material Symbols / Phosphor / Tabler). Currently the visitor sees system glyph fallback which doesn't really demonstrate the brand-consistent-icons motivation.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate `text` + `color` interplay
openQuestions.detail: The page mentions `color` works freely under font-variant-emoji: text, but never demonstrates it. Add a colour-picker that recolours the monochrome glyphs and watch the colour stick under text/unicode but get ignored under emoji.

summary: Hands-on demo with a live-edit input — strong base. Missing chromestatus reference link and the icon-font landing surface that would make the value proposition concrete.
